game_name: The Simpsons: Bart vs. the World

danger_signals.visual_form: Mr. Burns family members and agents appearing in stages
danger_signals.meaning: Hostile entities threaten Bart's progression; boss encounters signal major danger
Navigational threats require precise timing and skill to overcome

danger_signals.visual_form: Bart can take up to five hits before dying
danger_signals.meaning: Visual damage accumulation signals increasing vulnerability

safety_signals.meaning: Restorative items provide immediate safety buffer and recovery

safety_signals.visual_form: Cape item pickup transforms Bart into Bartman
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invulnerability and flight capability signals empowerment and safety window

safety_signals.visual_form: Other Simpson characters appear to provide hints
safety_signals.meaning: NPC guidance offers safe passage information and social safety

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Family members positioned at strategic points throughout stages; interstitial map appearances

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Localized Burns variants: Fu Manchu Burns (China), Abominable Snow Burns (North Pole), Ramses Burns (Egypt), Eric von Burns (Hollywood)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Boss encounters at stage conclusions; agents scattered throughout levels as enemies

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Souvenir collection objective visible through level design; items scattered across themed environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Bart navigates stages seeking collectibles; puzzle-solving for item discovery

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Burns family members deployed across global locations as bosses
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Staged encounters with increasingly dangerous adversaries in each geographic area

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Four distinct geographic areas (China, North Pole, Egypt, Hollywood) each with multiple stages
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Level progression structured by location; environmental design reflects real-world setting aesthetics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cape pickup triggers visual transformation to Bartman superhero form
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Flight capability and temporary invulnerability enable traversal of previously inaccessible areas

environmental_storytelling.example: Each of four areas (China, North Pole, Egypt, Hollywood) has distinct visual and gameplay characteristics reflecting real-world settings
Other Simpson family members positioned throughout levels to provide contextual guidance on souvenir locations
Regional Burns variants (Fu Manchu, Abominable Snow, Ramses, Eric von) serve as location identifiers and climactic narrative punctuation
Mandatory Krusty-brand souvenir collection gates progression to bonus content and optimal ending
Puzzle and trivia minigames embedded in areas; trivia based on show episodes creates narrative continuity

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Transforms into Bartman; gains flight capability for limited duration; enhanced traversal
Health indicator depletes toward death threshold (5-hit limit)
Staged combat with unique attack patterns specific to location (Fu Manchu, Snow Burns, Ramses, Eric variants)
Positioned at strategic locations to communicate souvenir information and environmental guidance

notes: This is a licensed platformer with strong narrative framing derived from the Simpsons IP. Visual communication relies heavily on iconic character design, location theming, and collectible-driven progression. The world-state is communicated through geographic diversification (four distinct areas) and character faction positioning (Burns family as distributed antagonists). The game uses environmental storytelling through stage design, NPC placement, and boss variants tied to locations. Limited mechanical complexity means danger/safety signals rely on visual item pickup design and enemy sprite appearance rather than subtle environmental cues. The souvenir mechanic serves as the primary world-state indicator (scavenger hunt progress).
